title: AGF Gamefeel components
description: Component definitions for the gamefeel domain. Pulled in by scene.schema.json via $ref; inlined into the bundled schema by engine/tools/schemas/load-scene-schema.ts.

tweensComponent.description: M19-tween: array of in-progress tweens on this entity. Each tween writes `from→to` into another component's field over `duration` seconds using `ease`. `loop: "none"` removes the tween on completion; `"loop"` resets `elapsed`; `"ping-pong"` flips from/to. Replay-safe: TweenSystem runs in fixedUpdate so the same elapsed value is reproduced across `engine replay`.

component.description: Sibling component name to write into, e.g. 'Transform' or 'MeshRenderer'.

property.description: Field name on the target component, e.g. 'scale' or 'opacity'.

ease.description: `pulse` uses sin(π·t) so a one-shot tween animates base → peak → base and auto-removes on completion (one-shot bounces / glints / damage flashes).

elapsed.description: Time accumulated so far. TweenSystem advances this each fixed step.

waypointMoverComponent.description: M19-waypoint-mover: generic primitive that moves the entity through a list of world-space positions. Sibling of CinematicCamera but writes plain Transform (not just the active camera) and derives yaw from velocity when `faceForward: true`. Replay-safe: WaypointMoverSystem runs in fixedUpdate.

duration.description: Seconds to traverse from the previous waypoint to this one.

faceForward.description: When true, writes Transform.rotation = [0, yaw, 0] each step with yaw derived from the velocity vector so the entity's local -Z points along the path. Matches three.js camera convention.

particleEmitterComponent.description: M19-particle-preset: spawn particles from a project-declared preset. Renderer adapter draws via additive InstancedMesh of small quads; particle data stays ECS-side so save/load survives. `preset` references `examples/<project>/particles/<id>.particle.json`. `lifetime: 0` keeps the emitter alive indefinitely (manual stop via applyCommands removing the component).

preset.description: Preset id matching a project particle manifest.

lifetime.description: Seconds the emitter stays alive; the component is removed once elapsed >= lifetime. 0 = forever.

inputActionComponent.description: S098 AGF-PROBE-INPUT-INJECT: engine-side transient written by runtime.injectInput (or directly via POST /__agf/input/action). Project input systems read + remove it each frame, translating the abstract action name into project-specific transients (e.g. 'place-bomb' → PlaceBombRequest in Kaboom Crew). Engine stays project-agnostic; new actions don't require schema changes. `value` is optional free-form JSON the action handler interprets.

action.description: Abstract action identifier. Conventional names used by examples/kaboom-crew: 'place-bomb', 'restart', 'move-up', 'move-down', 'move-left', 'move-right', 'stop'. Projects can grow new verbs without engine changes.

value.description: Optional payload the action handler interprets. JSON-compatible.
